title: Viola oxyceras
intro: Viola oxyceras is a species of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae), characterized by its distinctive, heart-shaped leaves and delicate purple flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Viola oxyceras include iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Viola oxyceras is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues, skin problems, and stress-related disorders.
taxonomy: Viola oxyceras is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. It is a member of the genus Viola, which comprises around 400 to 500 species of plants. Viola oxyceras is native to China and is part of the larger Viola genus, which is widely distributed across the Northern Hemisphere. The plant is a perennial herb, characterized by its distinctive leaves and flowers. Within the Violaceae family, Viola oxyceras is part of the tribe Violeae.
